trajectory.description: Observation: Starting from scratch on DomainNet-345 DG task with Quickdraw as target domain. Published baselines cap around 17% accuracy. Analysis: Need a strong OOD backbone (CLIP ViT-B/16) with proper multi-GPU training, standard augmentation plus grayscale augmentation to bridge the domain gap to Quickdraw's black-stroke-on-white style. Decision: Build complete pipeline with CLIP ViT-B/16, DDP training, 5 epochs for baseline, then submit eval to measure iteration time and get initial score.

Observation: 20.73% accuracy with CLIP ViT-B/16 after 5 epochs - already above published baselines (~17%). Val accuracy still climbing (81.5%), so more training will help. Iteration took ~25min for training + ~25min for eval. Analysis: Key improvements: (1) train longer (15 epochs), (2) add grayscale augmentation to bridge domain gap to Quickdraw's black-stroke style, (3) add test-time augmentation in predict.py, (4) cosine LR schedule. Decision: First check current code, then update with improvements and train for 15 epochs.

Val accuracy was still climbing (81.5%), indicating room for improvement. Analysis: Key improvements needed: (1) EMA for better OOD generalization, (2) Mixup regularization, (3) Stronger augmentation (more grayscale, color inversion, Gaussian blur) to bridge domain gap to Quickdraw's black-stroke style, (4) More training epochs (15), (5) TTA at prediction time (center crop + horizontal flip). Decision: Implement all improvements and train for 15 epochs, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Accuracy improved from 20.73% to 21.17% with CLIP ViT-B/16 after 15 epochs with EMA+Mixup. Val accuracy still climbing (81.5%), indicating room for improvement. Analysis: Key improvements to try: (1) SWAD (Stochastic Weight Averaging Densely) - proven to significantly improve DomainNet DG by finding flat minima, (2) More aggressive grayscale augmentation (p=0.5) to bridge domain gap to Quickdraw's black-stroke style, (3) More color inversion (p=0.2), (4) Add RandomSolarize and RandomErasing, (5) Train longer (25 epochs), (6) Use SWAD/EMA weights in predict.py instead of regular model weights. Decision: Implement SWAD + better augmentation + longer training, then submit eval with SWAD checkpoint.

The val_acc is slightly lower than the EMA run (0.8115 vs 0.8154) but OOD performance may differ. Decision: Submit eval for current SWAD checkpoint, then plan DINOv2 + edge detection augmentation for next iteration.

Multiple AugReg training attempts failed due to truncated scripts causing DDP crashes (syntax errors in incomplete files). Analysis: Root cause was truncated train_augreg.py. Decision: Write complete, concise train_augreg.py using DataParallel (single process, multi-GPU), train AugReg ViT-B/16 for 8 epochs with SWAD + mixup + strong augmentation, then submit eval with ensemble in next iteration.
